person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Father Sir William le Boteler, 1st Lord Boteler of Wem b. 11 Jun 1274, d. c 14 Sep 1334

Mother Beatrice d. bef 22 Nov 1306

Sir William 'the Elder' le Boteler, 2nd Lord Boteler was born on 8 September 1298. He was of Wemme, Whixall, & Hinstock, Shropshire, England.

He married Margaret FitzAlan, daughter of Sir Richard FitzAlan, 8th Earl Arundel, Lord Clun and Oswaldestre and Alisone de Saluzzo, circa 1325; They had 1 son (Sir William, 3rd Lord Boteler).

Sir William 'the Elder' le Boteler, 2nd Lord Boteler married Joan de Sudeley, daughter of John de Sudeley, 2nd Lord Sudeley and Eleanor de Scales, circa 1354; They had 1 son (Sir Thomas, 4th Lord Sudeley).

Sir William 'the Elder' le Boteler, 2nd Lord Boteler died on 22 December 1361 at age 63.

«b»Family 1«/b»
Margaret FitzAlan d. b 1354

«b»Child«/b»
1.) Sir William le Botiller, 3rd Lord le Botiller of Wem and Oversley b. c 1331, d. 14 Aug 1369

«b»Family 2«/b»
Joan de Sudeley d. b 11 Aug 1367

«b»Child«/b»
1.) Sir Thomas Boteler, 4th Baron Sudeley b. 1 Oct 1358, d. 21 Sep 1398

zh.lifeSummary: 當William le Boteler於1298年9月8日出生在英國史洛普爾時，他的父親Ralph le Boteler26歲，母親Hawise Gobion15歲。他於大約1350年與Joan Sudeley在英國英格蘭史洛普爾維姆結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1361年12月22日在他的家鄉享年63歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William le Boteler于1298年9月8日出生在英国史洛普爾时，他的父亲Ralph le Boteler26岁，母亲Hawise Gobion15岁。他于大约1350年与Joan Sudeley在英国英格兰史洛普爾維姆结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1361年12月22日在他的家乡享年63岁。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Boteler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a maker of leather bottles, from an agent derivative of Middle English botel, Old French bouteille ‘bottle’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When William le Boteler was born on 8 September 1298, in Shropshire, England, his father, Ralph le Boteler, was 26 and his mother, Hawise Gobion, was 15. He married Joan Sudeley about 1350, in Wem, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 22 December 1361, in his hometown, at the age of 63.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William le Boteler est né le 8 septembre 1298, à Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Ralph le Boteler, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Hawise Gobion, avait 15 ans. Il a épousé Joan Sudeley environ 1350, à Wem, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 22 décembre 1361, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 63 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als William le Boteler am 8. September 1298 in Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Ralph le Boteler 26 und seine Mutter Hawise Gobion 15. Er hat um 1350 in Wem, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom Joan Sudeley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 22. Dezember 1361 im Alter von 63 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William le Boteler è nato il il 8 settembre 1298 nel Shropshire, England, il suo padre, Ralph le Boteler, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Hawise Gobion, aveva 15 anni. Lui sposato Joan Sudeley circa 1350, nel Wem, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 22 dicembre 1361, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 63 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: William le Boteler(이)가 1298년 9월 8일 영국 슈럽실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ralph le Boteler(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Hawise Gobion(은)는 15세였다. 그는 대략 1350년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 베에서 Joan Sudeley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1361년 12월 22일 그의 고향에서 63세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.8 September 1298–22 December 1361: 8 de setembro de 1298–22 de dezembro de 1361
pt.lifeSummary: Quando William le Boteler nasceu em 8 de setembro de 1298, em Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Ralph le Boteler, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Hawise Gobion, tinha 15 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan Sudeley aproximadamente 1350, em Wem, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 22 de dezembro de 1361, em sua cidade natal, com 63 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William le Boteler родился 8 сентября 1298 г. в местности шропшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Ralph le Boteler было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Hawise Gobion, 15. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Sudeley около 1350 г. в местности вем, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 22 декабря 1361 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 63 лет.

es.8 September 1298–22 December 1361: 8 de septiembre de 1298–22 de diciembre de 1361
es.lifeSummary: Cuando William le Boteler nació el 8 de septiembre de 1298, en Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Ralph le Boteler, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Hawise Gobion, tenía 15 años. Se casó con Joan Sudeley aproximadamente 1350, en Wem, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 22 de diciembre de 1361, en su pueblo, a la edad de 63 años.
